summary: Iteration 2 complete: 15/15 new endpoint tests pass (Google session, Avatar update validation, regression). 22/24 of previous regression tests still pass (1 pre-existing flaky test, 1 skipped by design). Frontend mobile flows all verified: BARCODIA rebrand, Google login buttons, bottom tabs visible & navigable, avatar picker with 8 presets + upload + save, scan manual fallback, inventory, Trading House (Barcodia Marketplace).

minor.issue: When upstream Emergent returns 404 for bogus session, our handler returns 401 with detail 'Google session invalid'. Behavior correct. (Documented for clarity only.)

integration_issues.issue: Same pre-existing flaky web-only timing issue from iter 1: scan-reveal modal sometimes not detectable via Playwright wait_for_selector after manual entry submit. Backend POST /api/scan succeeds and item is persisted (verified previously via inventory). Not blocking.

issues: Persistent (non-blocking) console deprecation warnings: shadow*, textShadow* style props and props.pointerEvents. Migrate to boxShadow / textShadow / style.pointerEvents.

Avatar presets include what appears to be a Spider-Man image (preset:5). User asked for 'anime/fantasy' avatars; Spider-Man is a Marvel IP and not really fantasy/anime. Consider replacing with a licensed/royalty-free fantasy or anime portrait.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Replace Spider-Man preset image (preset:5) with a fantasy/anime portrait to match user's stated theme.
OPTIONAL (pre-existing): Address React Native web deprecation warnings (shadow*, textShadow*, pointerEvents).

Pre-existing flaky test: backend_test.py::test_upgrade_rejects_past_char_level intermittently passes/fails depending on user XP grind; iter 1 already documented this. Consider isolating with a fresh user each run.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 705 lines (was 611). Crossed the 700-line threshold. Time to split into routes/auth.py, routes/items.py, routes/market.py, routes/scan.py.
Google session handler does external httpx call without rate-limiting; could be abused. Acceptable for MVP but consider basic per-IP throttle in production.

Avatar size limit is enforced AFTER pydantic accepts arbitrarily large JSON. For very large payloads (e.g., 10MB), FastAPI will still parse them before the 1.5MB check. Consider a request body size limit middleware.
Market buy flow remains non-atomic (gold transfer + item ownership are separate Mongo ops) — pre-existing comment from iter 1.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_new_endpoints.py (created with 15 pytest cases: Google session, Avatar CRUD/validation, regression smoke)

success_rate.backend: 100% on new endpoints (15/15). Regression 22/24 pass, 1 skipped, 1 pre-existing flaky.
success_rate.frontend: 100% on iteration-2 requirements (rebrand, Google buttons, tabs, avatar picker, market subtitle, scan fallback).

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_av_*, TEST_buyer_*, TEST_seller_*, TEST_cancel_*, TEST_up_*, TEST_poor_* prefixed users via /api/auth/register. Seed user test@bq.com avatar now 'preset:5' after frontend save test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-2 changes verified working: (1) BARCODIA title on login/register; (2) login-google + register-google buttons exist & clickable (cannot complete OAuth in headless); (3) Bottom tab testIDs tab-character/tab-scan/tab-inventory/tab-market all render and navigate correctly to /character /scan /inventory /market; (4) Avatar picker (testID avatar-edit-btn -> avatar-picker modal) shows 8 presets (avatar-preset:1..8), avatar-upload, avatar-save; selecting preset + save calls PUT /api/auth/avatar and persists; (5) Market subtitle 'Barcodia Marketplace' rendered as CSS uppercase 'BARCODIA MARKETPLACE'; tab IDs are market-tab-browse / market-tab-my (NOT 'tab-browse'); (6) Scan manual fallback: scan-manual-open-fallback -> scan-manual-input -> scan-manual-submit. scan-reveal modal still flaky on web (known iter-1 issue, functional). Backend new tests in /app/backend/tests/test_new_endpoints.py.
rca_of_issue: No new bugs. The single regression failure (test_upgrade_rejects_past_char_level) is the same pre-existing flaky test documented in iter 1: when AI flavor calls speed up, the user gains XP faster from prior scans and levels up; the test then finds a level-1 item but user is level 2+, so the upgrade endpoint correctly succeeds (returns 200) instead of rejecting. This is a test-design issue, not a production bug. Mitigation: rewrite the test to register a NEW user each time and immediately attempt upgrade on a single scanned item before any XP grind.
